Milkshake Mania Wiki
Play
Wiki / Research

Research tree

The Research Lab holds two things. The XP tracks (Production, Marketing, Operations) open at Level 6: spend XP across 88 nodes, each a one-time unlock. The money-funded Flavor Lab opens right away at Level 1 with 37 more. Bonuses compound with all your other multipliers. Research is not instant — each unlock takes a number of in-game days, and each lab runs one project at a time.

New in v1.4: each track holds a different number of skill trees, all shown together in one frame — Production has 2 (25 nodes), Marketing 3 (30), Operations 4 (33). Trees flow left → right by tier and stack as bands on desktop; on phones in portrait they sit side by side and flow top → bottom. On desktop a full-screen button opens the whole tree with room to breathe. The trees are deliberately irregular: tiers vary in width, whole trees can start mid-way (the Digital tree starts at tier 2, Innovation at tier 3), lineages merge within a tree, and edges can even cross trees (dashed connectors) — e.g. Factory Planet needs the finales of BOTH production trees. The shapes are organic, not symmetric — long spines with offshoots and dead-end side branches, with only the occasional convergence. There's a light fog of war: you can see 5 tiers ahead, but only the next 2 are named — the three beyond show as “?” nodes, and deeper ones reveal as you progress.

  • Production (2 trees): Precision (blend speed → … → Nano-Blenders → Perfect Pour → Factory Planet) & Supply Chain (workforce → … → Assembly Line → Supply Singularity). Factory Planet merges both trees.
  • Marketing (3 trees): Growth (fan favorites → … → Viral Loop → Cultural Phenomenon), Reach (goldens → … → Brand Empire → Household Name) & Digital (Shake App → analytics/streamers → AI targeting → Virtual Shakes → Omnichannel, which also needs Viral Loop).
  • Operations (4 trees): Cost Control (hiring → … → Franchising → Operational Perfection), Scale (catering → … → Megacorp → Milkshake Monopoly), Automation (kiosks → robo-baristas/IoT → self-healing → Lights-Out Shops, which also needs Staff Scheduling) & Innovation (Skunkworks Lab → patents/moonshots → Breakthrough, which also needs Megacorp).
Flavor Lab · New in v1.4

Flavor Development

A fourth tab in the Research Lab, funded with money instead of XP and open from Level 1. It upgrades the flavor system itself — not which flavors you own — and runs on its own project queue, so it can develop alongside the XP tracks. Four interacting trees, 37 one-time unlocks, same tree UI, fog of war and full-screen view as the XP tracks.

TreeWhat it doesSignature node
Blender EngineeringMix more flavors at once (slot ceiling 5 → 7); Full-Blender Harmonics pays out per active flavor.Dual-Chamber Blending (+2 slots)
Ingredient ScienceMake every flavor hit harder — and Hyper-Concentration eases the diminishing-returns penalty on a stacked lineup.Molecular Emulsification (+15% flavor power)
Customer PsychologyMake flavors sell for more; Flavor Mastery rewards your whole collection (+income per unlocked flavor).Flavor Mastery, Scent Priming, Desire Engine
Flavor SynergyAmplify combos and unlock cross-flavor bonuses.Synergistic Pairing (Combos +10%)

Mod synergy. Two nodes reward installed flavor mods directly: Open Flavor Chemistry makes modded flavors far more potent, and the Open Recipe API pays an income dividend for every modded flavor you serve. Flavor Development also adds three new modding hook points.

Most other nodes give +flavor income that pools into your overall multiplier. It opens at level 1, so costs start around $150 at the roots — affordable within a few blends — and climb into the tens of millions on the deepest nodes, so there's always a next project no matter your bankroll.

Track 1

Production

Faster blends, bigger yields, more specials, leaner supply. 2 trees, 25 nodes — Precision and Supply Chain — ending in the Nano-Blenders / Assembly Line capstones, deep tails (Perfect Pour / Supply Singularity), and one final cross-tree merge: Factory Planet requires the finale of both trees.

Signature nodeBranchEffect (L1 / L2)
Blend OptimizationPrecision−10% / −18% blend time
Yield BoostPrecision+15% / +28% manual income
Special BatchesPrecision+8% / +15% special chance
Workforce EfficiencySupply Chain+8% / +15% passive income
Bulk ProcurementSupply Chain+5% / +10% bulk discount

Every other node is a one-time +global income unlock (from ~+2% at the roots up to ~+10% on the deepest nodes). XP cost and research time scale with tier depth — roughly 150 XP / 10 days at the roots up to ~12,000 XP / 50 days on the final tail.

Track 2

Marketing

More fan favorites and goldens, bigger combo & country multipliers, faster XP.

3 trees, 30 nodes — Growth and Reach (12 each, ending in Viral Loop → Cultural Phenomenon and Brand Empire → Household Name) plus the staggered Digital tree (6 nodes, starts at tier 2: Shake App → … → Omnichannel, a cross-tree merge that also needs Viral Loop).

Signature nodeBranchEffect (L1 / L2)
Fan Favorite PushGrowth+6% / +12% fan-favorite rate
Staff DevelopmentGrowth+15% / +30% XP gains
Combo MasteryGrowthCombos +8% / +15%
Golden FormulaReach+3% / +6% golden chance
Global OutreachReachCountry mult +5% / +10%

Every other node is a one-time +global income unlock (up to ~+10% on the deepest nodes), with XP cost and research time rising by tier depth.

Track 3

Operations

Cheaper hires & upgrades, bigger event & contract rewards, lower tax.

4 trees, 33 nodes — Cost Control and Scale (12 each, ending in Franchising → Operational Perfection and Megacorp → Milkshake Monopoly) plus two staggered trees: Automation (5 nodes from tier 1: kiosks → … → Lights-Out Shops, also needs Staff Scheduling) and Innovation (4 nodes from tier 3: Skunkworks Lab → Breakthrough, also needs Megacorp).

Signature nodeBranchEffect (L1 / L2)
Hiring PipelineCost Control−12% / −22% hire cost
Cost ReductionCost Control−8% / −15% upgrade cost
Crisis ManagementCost Control+15% / +30% event rewards
Catering ExpertiseScale+25% / +45% contract reward
Tax OptimizationScaleTax −5% / −10%

Every other node is a one-time +global income unlock (up to ~+10% on the deepest nodes), with XP cost and research time rising by tier depth.

Research is paid in XP, the same currency that drives your level — but spending it never lowers your level (level tracks lifetime XP). Plan around which bonuses move your numbers most.

Leveling & XPWhere XP comes from Shops & upgradesMoney upgrades
